gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) version 1.0.0-seed has all six articles undefined. Arbiter (#190) verifies the BoR at every scope query, but with no substantive articles, there is no enterprise perimeter to enforce. The DIO currently has no constitutional restrictions on its activities, leaving it free to operate outside of any intended scope.

The MSP is in bootstrap seed state with zero rules. Nomos (#200) evaluates MSP + genome compliance at every Action Proposal adjudication, but the empty rule set means all proposals pass by default. The DIO operates under default-permissive evaluation, nullifying the second compliance gate.

The BoR can only be amended by the human principal. No human authorship activity has been recorded since at least 2026-04-02 (last Minder observation of Leon). The governance core remains in scaffold state, blocking all constitutional operations.

The Senate (#220) holds authority to write MSP rules, but the MSP remains empty 23 days after boot. No Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) have triggered rule drafts, and no Senate-Legislator activity is evident. The DIO lacks operational guardrails.

The MSP genome section is empty, meaning no organ-specific operating rules or risk-tier constraints exist. Organs are running without explicit, evaluated constraints, increasing the risk of misbehavior or conflicting actions between organs.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-001",
      "grounding_msp_section_or_bor_article": "Bill of Rights — All Articles (Articles I–VI are scaffold placeholders with no content)",
      "description": "The BoR lacks any codified relational principles: no scope of enterprise, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, amendment protocol, or interpretation rules. This means there is no constitutional definition of who the DIO serves, what relational obligations it has toward the human principal (Leon) or other persons, how human authority is exercised, or what interactions are forbidden. Without this, the Arbiter cannot enforce a relational perimeter, and the MSP cannot translate relational boundaries into actionable rules. The system operates in a default-permissive mode that is disconnected from any explicit person-attention mandate.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "severe",
      "priority_score": 100
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-002",
      "grounding_msp_section_or_bor_article": "MSP — Rules section ('The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation…')",
      "description": "The MSP has no relational rules (e.g., obligations to consult the human principal, constraints on autonomous action, requirements to seek clarification before acting). Because the BoR is empty, the MSP cannot inherit relational constraints; the result is a complete absence of machine-evaluable person-attention protocols. Nomos has no compliance gate for relational behavior, leaving the DIO unguided on when to defer to human judgment, how to manage conflicts of intent, or how to respect decision boundaries between governance branches.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 80
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-003",
      "grounding_msp_section_or_bor_article": "MSP — Relationship to the Bill of Rights ('The MSP is strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights…')",
      "description": "This relationship is declared but non-functional because the BoR contains no relational substance. The clause that an MSP rule conflicting with the BoR is void on its face has no effect. A key relational missing component is that no BoR article defines what constitutes a conflict in the context of person-attention, and thus no guardrail exists to prevent the MSP from inadvertently authorizing actions that would disrespect human authority or relational expectations. The intention of subordination is stated but inoperable.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority_score": 70
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-004",
      "grounding_msp_section_or_bor_article": "MSP — Genome References ('Genome documents live under genome/… They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. — (none at seed)')",
      "description": "No genome documents exist, so there are no organ-specific relational rules (e.g., how the Minder sector should maintain peer observations, update attention models, or enforce conversational protocols). The observations recorded in Minder/Hippocampus are currently unconstrained by any declarative relational policy; they are purely aspirational. Without genome rules, the system cannot enforce relational invariants such as frequency of attention updates, confidentiality boundaries, or escalation triggers when human intent appears to shift.",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority_score": 50
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-005",
      "grounding_msp_section_or_bor_article": "BoR — Article IV (Human Authority Reservation, undefined)",
      "description": "The Minder slice shows active peer 'urn:minder:peer:leon' as a human and a persona 'urn:minder:peer:claude', but there is no constitutional statement that Leon is the human principal, that his authority is reserved, or that the persona has only delegated agency. The observations focus on deducing Leon's technical/architectural preferences, but not on capturing his authority models, consent protocols, or when the DIO must pause for affirmation. This gap means that person-attention coverage lacks a constitutional anchor; the system has no way to distinguish advisory observations from binding directives, and it cannot enforce that Leon's status as sole human authority is recognized across all organs.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 95
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-006",
      "grounding_msp_section_or_bor_article": "BoR — Article II (Prohibited Activities, undefined)",
      "description": "The recent observations are almost entirely about Leon's four IP-protected projects and his architectural constraints, yet there is no constitutional prohibition on the DIO exposing, copying, or reusing project-specific concepts outside their contractual guardrails. Even though system observations note that IP protection is contractual, the BoR lacks a relational prohibition against the DIO itself violating those protections or inadvertently leaking cross-project boundaries. Without an explicit prohibition, the system's person-attention coverage toward the human's interests is incomplete and exposes relational risk (loss of trust, breach of authorial intent).",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "critical",
      "priority_score": 90
    }
  ]
}

```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "SGA-001",
      "msp_section": "MSP Rules (currently none)",
      "bor_article": "BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise [undefined]",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority": 125,
      "evidence": [
        "MSP version 1.0.0-seed — Rules section: '(none)' — explicit default-permissive evaluation active",
        "BoR all six Articles marked '[To be defined by human principal]' — no constitutional perimeter exists",
        "Memory block b5004658: 'Probabilistic services contain internal service agents and/or service orchestrators' — platform assigning LLM authority without scope constraints",
        "Memory block 817a964c: Autoheal Layer 3 fixer built with '6 safety guardrails' but guardrails are self-defined, not constitutionally grounded — 'Human approval required for all code changes' is an implementation choice, not a BoR mandate",
        "Memory block 601c42ea: 'The human is just another consumer' — stated in architectural vision with no BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) to constrain this flattening"
      ],
      "why_strategic": "The entire governance stack (Arbiter #190, Nomos #200, Senate #220) depends on a defined BoR for scope enforcement. Arbiter verifies BoR at every scope query — with all Articles undefined, the scope gate is vacuously permissive. MSP operates in default-permissive mode. Every architectural decision currently being made (organ structure, Autoheal code-change authority, Spine event bus, probabilistic service scope) is unconstrained by constitutional boundaries. The platform is building its own constitution implicitly through implementation — this is a fundamental inversion: the BoR should constrain what gets built, not be retrofitted to what was already built."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "SGA-002",
      "msp_section": "MSP Purpose — 'machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules'",
      "bor_article": null,
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority": 80,
      "evidence": [
        "Radiant stats: recent_goals = [] — empty array, zero strategic objectives dispatched",
        "MSP Purpose states Nomos evaluates every Action Proposal against 'objectives, invariants, constraints' — with no MSP rules and no goals, evaluation is vacuous",
        "All 16 recent context blocks are operational artifacts (backup results, dream cycle reports) — zero strategic initiative context",
        "Memory shows deep architectural work (28-organ DIO, governance organs MP-8 through MP-11) but no memory entry links any build decision to an MSP strategic objective",
        "Recent Goals anti-redundancy hint is empty — system has no awareness of what it was trying to accomplish"
      ],
      "why_strategic": "The MSP is designed to translate BoR into actionable strategic objectives. With both MSP rules empty and zero goals dispatched, the DIO is operating entirely tactically — responding to operational events (backup failures, dream cycles) without any strategic vector. The platform is building sophisticated governance infrastructure (Arbiter, Nomos, Senate, Cerberus) but has no strategic objective defining WHY these organs exist or WHAT outcomes they serve. The empty recent_goals array means Senate has never dispatched a single OKR or longer-arc goal. This is the strategic equivalent of a body with organs but no brainstem — autonomous function without directed purpose."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "SGA-003",
      "msp_section": "MSP Amendment Protocol — 'Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos when an AP is blocked by current MSP/genome constraints'",
      "bor_article": "BoR Article V — Amendment Protocol [undefined]",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority": 60,
      "evidence": [
        "Radiant stats: dream cycle scheduled=disabled, health=never_run — but recent_context shows Dream Cycles #12-#15 completed, indicating health monitoring infrastructure is decoupled from actual execution",
        "Dream Cycle #13 Phase 2: 'deferred to next interactive Claude Code session' — memory consolidation requires human-initiated session, not autonomous execution",
        "Dream Cycle #15 Phase 2: 'MCP tools not available in scheduled task session. Consolidation performed via direct psql with equivalent logic. Semantic similarity scan skipped (no embedding sidecar access)' — degraded execution path",
        "Embedding coverage: 72% (69/96 with embeddings) — 27 memory blocks lack semantic indexing, degrading Radiant's ability to retrieve strategically relevant context",
        "Memory block 997e1a35: 'Dream cycle stale (3 days)' noted in Oracle Opinion #13 — chronic scheduling fragility acknowledged but never escalated to PEM"
      ],
      "why_strategic": "The dream cycle is the DIO's mechanism for converting operational context into strategic memory. Degraded dream execution means the platform cannot autonomously form the long-term memory that strategic reasoning depends on. Phase 2 (LLM-driven consolidation) is the step that synthesizes raw context into abstracted memory — when it's deferred or runs degraded (no embedding sidecar, direct psql fallback), the memory formation pipeline produces lower-quality consolidation. Over time, this creates a strategic amnesia: context blocks expire (7-day TTL visible in recent_context), and the strategic insights they contain are lost rather than consolidated into permanent memory. The MSP Amendment Protocol exists specifically so that operational blockages like this can trigger PEMs that drive systemic fixes — but the dream cycle degradation has persisted across multiple cycles without generating a PEM, suggesting Nomos isn't evaluating dream-cycle health as a compliance-relevant constraint."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "SGA-004",
      "msp_section": "MSP Genome References — 'extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints'",
      "bor_article": "BoR Article III — Core Identity [undefined]",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 3,

      "evidence": [
        "Memory entity distribution: llm-ops=53, coretex=8, graphheight=2, all others ≤1 — DIO's self-model is operationally distorted",

        "MSP Genome References section: '(none at seed)' — zero organ-specific operating rules exist for any of the 28 defined organs",
        "Memory block 817a964c: Autoheal Classifier 'self-evolving knowledge base (3 seed patterns)' and Fixer 'self-evolving fix-history.md (3 seed patterns)' — these are organ-level genome rules being created ad-hoc without MSP Genome framework"
      ],
      "why_strategic": "The DIO has architecturally defined itself as 28 organs but has no genome — no organ-specific operating rules that constrain what each organ may do. Memory formation is heavily biased toward operational infrastructure (llm-ops=53 memories) while strategic organs have near-zero memory presence. This means the DIO 'knows' its backup pipeline intimately but has almost no consolidated memory about what Arbiter does, what constraints Nomos enforces, or what Senate has decided. The MSP Genome References section exists specifically to house these organ-specific constraints, but it remains empty. Without genome rules, each organ's autonomous behavior (especially probabilistic services with LLM access) has no structural boundary beyond whatever the implementation happens to enforce. The entity imbalance in memory suggests Radiant is not forming strategic self-knowledge — it remembers operations, not identity."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "SGA-005",
      "msp_section": "MSP Relationship to the Bill of Rights — 'Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face'",
      "bor_article": "BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities [undefined]",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority": 60,
      "evidence": [
        "BoR Article II (Prohibited Activities): '[To be defined by human principal]' — no activities are prohibited",
        "Memory block 07ad342f: 'Bus-driven reactive architecture adopted... Intelligence resides in services, not in message passing. Services may be deterministic or probabilistic' — architectural decision places LLM-capable services on the event bus with no prohibition framework",
        "Memory block e624cde5: 'Meta-Meta-Prompt... 15 meta prompts across 7 tiers, estimated ~95 relay prompts total' — massive autonomous build program with zero prohibited-activity guardrails",
        "Memory block 817a964c: CAEB/Spine elevation built with WebSocket push, subscription management, heartbeat — bidirectional communication infrastructure with no prohibition on what events may flow or which services may subscribe",
        "MSP 'Relationship to BoR' section establishes BoR supremacy but the supremacy clause has no substance to reference — it's a null pointer"
      ],
      "why_strategic": "The BoR Article II (Prohibited Activities) is the constitutional 'thou shalt not' — the hard boundaries that no organ, service, or Action Proposal may cross regardless of efficiency or convenience. Its absence means there is no activity the DIO is constitutionally forbidden from performing. The MSP's BoR-supremacy clause is technically active ('Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void') but operationally meaningless because there is no BoR to conflict with. Meanwhile, the platform has built an event bus (Spine) that routes messages between services, an Autoheal system that can propose and execute code changes, and a relay prompt pipeline that decomposes meta-prompts into ~95 autonomous build tasks — all without a single prohibited activity on the books. This is the strategic equivalent of building a power grid with no 'do not cross' lines on any transformer."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "SGA-006",
      "msp_section": "MSP Amendment Protocol — 'Senate-Legislator drafts the amendment as a minimal diff... Activation requires POST /amend/review with decision=approve'",
      "bor_article": "BoR Article IV — Human Authority Reservation [undefined]",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority": 60,
      "evidence": [
        "MSP Amendment Protocol specifies HOM notification to human-principal mailbox for approval — but recent_context shows zero PEMs generated despite documented operational failures",
        "Radiant recent_context: 7 backup result context blocks showing pattern of NAS failures since 2026-04-07, zombie processes (PID 27857, 28950 alive ~25h), script bugs (ORGAN_SQLITE_DBS unbound variable) — none triggered MSP amendment PEMs",
        "Memory block 8e6c090a: 'Pattern: NAS availability intermittent since 2026-04-07. AOS-coretex-rtime non-fast-forward divergence recurring in git sync' — pattern recognized and consolidated into memory but never escalated to governance",
        "Memory block 307ec510: GitLab sync failures on 2 occurrences same day — consolidated into memory as pattern recognition, no PEM generated",
        "Memory block 3eb7808b: Self-referential symlink bug — 'Remediation pipeline could not fix it because both fix_symlinks and handler_symlink_redeploy only ran the symlink deployer' — self-healing gap identified, fixed ad-hoc, no PEM to drive MSP rule addition",
        "MSP 'Amendment Protocol' section specifies PEMs as the ONLY originating trigger for MSP amendments — if PEMs aren't being generated, the MSP can never evolve beyond seed"
      ],
      "why_strategic": "The MSP Amendment Protocol creates a closed loop: operational blockages → PEMs → Senate-Legislator drafts → human approval → MSP evolution. This loop is broken at the first step. Seven backup failures, recurring GitLab sync issues, a self-referential symlink that evaded the remediation pipeline, and zombie processes persisting 25+ hours — all recognized by the system, some consolidated into memory as patterns — but none generated a PEM. Without PEMs, the MSP remains permanently at 1.0.0-seed with zero rules. The DIO is accumulating operational scar tissue (recognized failure patterns in memory) but not converting that scar tissue into immune system rules (MSP constraints). This gap is urgent because the Amendment Protocol is the ONLY path to MSP evolution — if it's not functioning, the entire governance maturation pathway is blocked regardless of when the BoR is authored."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "SGA-007",
      "msp_section": "MSP Purpose — 'Nomos (#200) evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication... An AP must pass both gates to be authorized'",
      "bor_article": "BoR Article VI — Interpretation Rules [undefined]",
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority": 45,
      "evidence": [
        "MSP specifies two compliance gates: Gate 1 (Arbiter scope → BoR perimeter), Gate 2 (Nomos → MSP + genome compliance). Both gates currently vacuous — BoR undefined, MSP contains no rules",
        "Memory block bab3c3b5: '4 message types: OTM (daily-token technical), APM (action proposals), PEM (policy exceptions), ATM (authorization tokens)' — Action Proposals are a defined message type flowing through Spine, but with vacuous gates, every AP passes",
        "Memory block 601c42ea: DIO definition: 'coordinated network of specialized agents... Intelligence is mechanically separated from authority' — but authority (Nomos gate evaluation) has no substance to enforce",
        "Radiant recent_context shows zero Action Proposal context blocks — APs may not be flowing through Nomos at all, or are passing trivially without generating diagnostic context",
        "Memory block e624cde5: 15 meta-prompts decomposed into ~95 relay prompts — each relay prompt is effectively an Action Proposal with no gate evaluation"
      ],
      "why_strategic": "Nomos (#200) is the DIO's operational immune system — it evaluates every Action Proposal against constitutional (BoR) and statutory (MSP) constraints before authorizing execution. Both gates are currently open circuits. This means every relay prompt, every Autoheal fix proposal, every service initialization is authorized by default. The intelligence/authority separation articulated in the DIO vision is mechanically present (Nomos exists, Arbiter exists) but operationally absent (neither has rules to enforce). Article VI (Interpretation Rules) is the BoR article that would define how conflicts are resolved and how ambiguity is handled — without it, even if Articles I-V were defined, there would be no framework for resolving edge cases. This gap has high impact because everything the DIO does flows through these gates — the gates are the single point of constitutional enforcement for the entire platform."
    }
  ]
}
```
